title: Economic value of nature has increased by 50 percent in one decade
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports a 50% rise in the economic value of ecosystem services between 2013 and 2022. The study focuses on long-term socio-economic valuation of nature, especially cultural services like tourism and recreation, without indicating short-term impacts on energy or gas demand.

title: Consumer confidence continues to decline
summary: Netherlands consumer confidence declined further in February, with CBS reporting the sentiment index at -32 versus -28 in January, reflecting increased pessimism about the economic climate and willingness to buy, but without immediate implications for gas demand or market tightness in the next two weeks.

title: US ranks as third-largest export market for steel
summary: Trade statistics on Dutch steel exports to the US; no near-term impact on Dutch or Northwest European gas demand, supply tightness, or power-sector gas burn identified.

title: Dutch exports to China up again while Germany’s have fallen
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports medium‑term trade shifts in Dutch and German exports to China; no link to near‑term energy demand, gas supply, or system conditions is indicated.

title: Unemployment rate at its most stable since 1988
summary: Dutch unemployment and vacancy statistics for 2024 are historically stable; article is a retrospective labour-market overview without near-term gas demand or supply implications.

title: Household consumption up by almost 2 percent in December
summary: CBS reports December 2024 Dutch household consumption up 1.8% year-on-year, including slightly higher energy-related spending. January consumption conditions appear more unfavourable, but the data are macro and retrospective, with no specific short-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Exports down by over 1 percent in December
summary: CBS reports December 2024 Dutch exports and imports fell 1.1% year on year, including lower crude oil and natural gas trade; February export conditions are described as less unfavourable, but this is retrospective macro trade data with no specific near-term gas market implications.

title: Dutch economy grows by 0.4 percent in Q4 2024
summary: Macro GDP and trade data for Q4 2024, including lower gas imports and use of reserves, but reported as historic statistics without indicating current or near-term supply stress.

title: Inflation decreases to 3.3 percent in January
summary: Netherlands CPI inflation fell to 3.3% in January 2025, from 4.1% in December, mainly due to lower energy prices and cheaper bungalow park accommodation linked to fewer holiday-period bookings.

title: Exports of horses worth 457 million euros in 2024
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports 2024 growth in Dutch live animal export value, led by horses, mainly to Germany and the US. No implications for near-term gas demand or energy-market tightness.

title: Number of bankruptcies remains the same in January
summary: Dutch statistics on January corporate bankruptcies show stable, relatively low levels versus 2024 and 2013 peak. Changes are concentrated in the trade sector and do not directly affect near‑term Dutch or Northwest European gas demand or supply tightness over the next two weeks.

title: No change in natural gas consumption in 2024
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports 2024 Dutch gas use was flat year-on-year: higher industrial demand, lower gas-for-power due to more renewables, and reduced LNG imports and exports. The article is backward-looking and does not indicate new conditions for the coming two weeks.

title: 172 thousand deaths in 2024
summary: Statistical report on Dutch mortality in 2024, noting more deaths than 2023 due mainly to an ageing population and differences by sex and age group. No near-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness are discussed.

title: Small area estimation of social cohesion indicators
summary: Methodological paper on statistical modelling of Dutch social cohesion indicators; no material link to gas demand, gas supply, storage, or energy-system conditions in the near term.

title: Manufacturing output down by over 4 percent in December
summary: Dutch manufacturing output fell 4.4% year-on-year in December 2024, extending an 18‑month contraction trend, with sentiment remaining negative in January.

title: International trade has tripled in past 30 years, even as growth has slowed
summary: Historical analysis of long‑term Dutch trade growth and shifts in trade with China; no near‑term implications for gas demand or Northwest European gas market tightness within the next two weeks.

title: 76 million airline passengers in 2024, 6 percent fewer than in 2019
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports 2024 Dutch air passenger and freight volumes versus previous years; this is historic traffic data with no immediate implications for near-term gas demand or market tightness.

title: Business confidence down in first quarter of 2025
summary: Dutch business confidence declined slightly in Q1 2025, with sentiment weakest in mining, quarrying, transportation and storage, while investment expectations for 2025 improved modestly.

title: Retail turnover up by 1.5 percent in December
summary: Dutch statistics office reports modest retail turnover growth for December 2024 and full-year 2024, with stronger non-food and weaker food sector sales and higher online turnover.

title: Nearly 6 in 10 Ukrainian refugees are in paid employment
summary: Statistical report on employment rates and contract types among Ukrainian refugees in the Netherlands; no near-term implications for gas demand, supply tightness, or energy system conditions.

title: Inflation was 3.3 percent in January according to flash estimate
summary: Dutch flash CPI estimate for January shows 3.3% year-on-year inflation, down from 4.1% in December. Month-on-month prices fell 0.3%. No specific information is provided on energy, gas, or weather-related demand factors.

title: Fewer asylum requests and more following family members in 2024
summary: Dutch statistics show a 16% drop in first-time asylum applications and a rise in family reunifications in 2024, with Syrians still the largest group. This is demographic/immigration data without implications for near-term gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Economic picture again less negative
summary: CBS reports that Dutch macroeconomic indicators remain below long-term trend, though slightly less negative, with modest year-on-year growth in consumption, exports, and investment. This is general business-cycle commentary without specific near-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Manufacturing output prices over 1 percent higher in December
summary: Dutch statistics office reports modest year-on-year rise in manufacturing output prices for December 2024, mainly reflecting smaller declines in crude oil and petroleum derivative prices. This is retrospective producer price data without direct short-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Manufacturers equally negative in January
summary: Dutch manufacturing confidence remained mildly negative in January, with modest changes in views on orders, output expectations and stocks. No direct short-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness are indicated.

title: Lower population growth in 2024
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports slower population growth in 2024 driven by migration trends. This is structural demographic information with no direct near-term impact on Dutch or Northwest European gas demand or supply conditions over the next two weeks.

title: Lowest wheat harvest in 25 years
summary: Statistical report on 2024 Dutch arable crop yields, highlighting a sharp decline in wheat production and minor changes in other crops. It is backward-looking and does not describe current energy, gas market, or weather conditions relevant to near-term gas demand.

title: Separation risks of married and cohabiting couples: How important is the social context?
summary: Sociological dissertation on separation risks among Dutch couples, focusing on social environment, religiosity, education, and financial resources. No link to energy or gas-market conditions.

title: Freight transport by Eastern European lorries increased sharply
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports retrospective 2023 data on freight volumes by international and Eastern European lorries to and from the Netherlands; no near-term gas market impact indicated.

title: Fewer early school leavers in the Netherlands than EU average
summary: Educational and childcare participation statistics in the Netherlands and EU for 2023; no implications for short-term gas demand or energy-market tightness.

title: Agricultural nitrogen surplus at lowest level since 1990
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports that Dutch agricultural nitrogen surplus in 2023 was the lowest since 1990, reflecting a long-term decline. This is historical environmental data with no direct short-term impact on gas demand or near-term gas market tightness.

title: Consumer confidence is deteriorating again
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports further deterioration in consumer confidence in January, mainly about the economic climate, but without specific implications for short-term gas demand or supply conditions.

title: Inflation down on Bonaire, up on St Eustatius and Saba
summary: Quarterly inflation data for Caribbean Netherlands islands, mainly driven by consumer prices and transport costs, with no clear link to near-term Dutch or Northwest European gas demand or gas-market tightness.

title: House prices nearly 11 percent up in December
summary: Dutch statistics report December 2024 house prices and transaction volumes, describing year-on-year changes in the housing market without any connection to gas demand, energy system conditions, or near-term gas market tightness.

title: Share of Dutch-owned multinationals in the economy declines
summary: Statistical report on the declining share of Dutch-owned multinationals in GDP and company counts; no near-term implications for Dutch or Northwest European gas demand or gas market tightness are identified.

title: Investment up by almost 4 percent in November
summary: Dutch statistics data on November 2024 investment volumes and a more unfavorable investment climate in December. This is macroeconomic commentary without direct near-term implications for Dutch or Northwest European gas demand or supply tightness.

title: A smart Travel Survey. What is the role of the respondent?
summary: Methodological study on smart travel surveys and respondent behavior using travel apps; no implications for near-term Dutch or Northwest European gas demand, supply tightness, or power-sector gas use.

title: Value of agricultural exports up by nearly 5 percent in 2024
summary: Report on 2024 Dutch agricultural export values and composition, driven mainly by price effects, with no indication of near-term impacts on gas demand, energy supply tightness, or system balancing conditions.

title: Unemployment unchanged at 3.7 percent in December
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports Dutch unemployment steady at 3.7% in December 2024, with slight rises in employment and labour participation. This is standard labour-market data with no direct short-term implications for gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Prices of new-build homes up 3.6 percent in Q3, year on year
summary: Dutch and EU housing price statistics for Q3 2024, including changes in new-build and existing home prices and transaction volumes, with no near-term impact on gas demand or supply conditions.

title: Exports up by over 4 percent in November
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports a 4.2% year-on-year increase in goods exports for November 2024 and slightly improved export conditions in January, reflecting general trade and industrial trends without specific implications for near-term Dutch or Northwest European gas market tightness or demand.

title: 56 percent of wealth in the hands of 10 percent of households
summary: Dutch statistics report on wealth distribution and inequality in 2023, with the top 10% holding 56% of assets. No direct link to near-term gas demand, supply tightness, or energy system conditions is indicated.

title: Inflation rate 3.3 percent in 2024
summary: Netherlands 2024 inflation averaged 3.3%, with housing rents and tobacco excise hikes key drivers. This is macroeconomic, not directly linked to short-term gas demand or supply conditions over the next two weeks.

title: Inflation increases to 4.1 percent in December
summary: Dutch statistics office reports December 2024 inflation at 4.1% year-on-year, with bungalow park accommodation prices contributing most to the increase. General CPI and holiday accommodation data have no direct short-term impact on gas demand or gas market tightness.

title: Bankruptcies up by over 30 percent in 2024
summary: Statistics Netherlands reports a 30% rise in Dutch bankruptcies in 2024 versus 2023, with a slight decline in December and no further increase during 2024. This is a retrospective macroeconomic update.

title: Manufacturing output remained virtually the same in November
summary: Dutch manufacturing output in November 2024 was flat year-on-year, extending a long stagnation trend; sentiment remains mildly negative. No immediate implications for gas demand or market tightness within the next two weeks are identified.

title: Statistical combination of different types of chlorofyll-a measurements in the Dutch North Sea
summary: Technical report on methods for combining chlorophyll-a measurements in the Dutch North Sea; no implications for near-term gas demand, supply tightness, or power-sector gas use.

title: Labour migrants with a partner in work are more likely to stay
summary: Statistics Netherlands study on labour and knowledge migrants’ stay duration between 2005-2016; historical demographics with no near‑term impact on gas demand or supply tightness.

title: Household consumption up by almost 1 percent in November
summary: CBS reports Dutch household consumption up 0.8% y/y in November 2024, including slightly higher spending on energy and other goods; December consumption conditions less favorable. Macro demand data only, no short-term signal for gas market tightness.

title: Inflation was 4.1% in December according to flash estimate
summary: Dutch statistics office reports December flash inflation at 4.1% CPI and 3.9% HICP, with full data due 14 January; macroeconomic update without direct short-term gas market impact.

title: Retail turnover up by over 2 percent in November
summary: Dutch November 2024 retail turnover data from CBS show modest year-on-year growth, led by non-food sales and weaker food sales. Article is macro-retail statistics without implications for near-term gas demand or energy-market tightness.

title: Manufacturers almost equally negative in December
summary: CBS reports Dutch manufacturing producer confidence stayed slightly negative in December, with modest changes in orders and sectoral sentiment. No direct short-term gas demand or supply impact is indicated.

title: Economic picture slightly more negative in November
summary: CBS business cycle data show Dutch macroeconomic indicators slightly weaker and confidence low in late 2024, with modest growth in consumption, exports and investment. This is routine macro commentary without specific near-term gas market implications.
